description: GatedAttn (per-head sigmoid gate on attention output, ported from @dexhunter PR #1736) stacked on our LoRA-TTT stack (alpha/rank scaling, warm-start A, WD=1.0, alpha=144). Two novel additions this submission: (a) matching gate application in the LoRA-TTT forward path (without it TTT diverges since scoring sees a different model than training), (b) per-row int8 quantization of the gate matrix to keep the artifact under 16MB while preserving gate precision. 3-seed mean 1.07081 BPB.
methods: LoRA-TTT stack (PR #1767 / this author): alpha/rank scaling, warm-start A across batches, TTT WD=1.0, alpha=144 on rank 128
GatedAttn ported from @dexhunter PR #1736 — per-head sigmoid gate on SDPA output before out_proj

NEW in this submission: mirror the gate application inside _block_with_lora and _parallel_block_with_lora (the TTT forward path reimplements attention inline; without the mirror, the scored distribution diverges from the training distribution and TTT collapses to ~1.40 BPB)

NEW in this submission: per-row int8 quantization for attn_gate_w weights (each of 11 gates is (num_heads, dim)=(8, 512); fp16 passthrough pushed artifact over 16MB). Per-row scale = one fp16 scalar per head, preserves precision — per-tensor int8 lost 0.00112 BPB, per-row int8 is back within 0.00024 of fp16.
Phased score-first TTT (3 phases, 2000 prefix docs) — dexhunter/romeerp
SP8192 + triple depth recurrence + parallel residuals + SD-clip GPTQ + VarLen + Fused MLP (unchanged)
